summary: Iter 165 fingerprint-verification gating (admin-controlled per employee, gated by firm_masters.salary_process.bio_matrix_attendance) — VERIFIED PASS with ONE minor UI bug. Backend: 7/7 pytest cases pass (test_iter165_fingerprint_gate.py) covering (a) initial firm bio_matrix_attendance=false precondition, (b) PATCH /api/admin/user-role {fingerprint_required:true} on bio-disabled firm → 400 with detail 'Fingerprint verification is not allowed — enable Bio Matrix Attendance for this firm in Firm Master first.', (c) same PATCH with fingerprint_required:false → 200 (turning OFF always allowed), (d) after flipping bio_matrix_attendance=true in Mongo, PATCH fingerprint_required:true → 200 and users.fingerprint_required=true, (e) employee TEST50/123456 pin-login + GET /api/auth/me returns firm_biometric_enabled=true, fingerprint_required=true, effective_fingerprint_required=true, (f) POST /api/me/fingerprint/enrolled {device:'web-pwa'} → 200 and users doc gets fingerprint_enrolled_at + fingerprint_device='web-pwa', (g) after flipping firm flag back to false, /auth/me shows firm_biometric_enabled=false and effective_fingerprint_required=false even though user-flag stays true. Frontend (admin desktop viewport 1440x900, super-admin session): /employee-master?user_id=user_44cd6f561da0 → Grouping card shows the new grouping-fingerprint-toggle row with 'Fingerprint verification: OFF' + hint 'Locked — enable Bio Matrix Attendance for this firm in Firm Master first.' at opacity 0.6, tapping does NOT flip state. After flipping bio_matrix_attendance=true in Mongo and reloading, the toggle unlocks (opacity 1, hint changes to normal helper), tapping flips ON, tapping Save grouping persists users.fingerprint_required=true (verified in Mongo). Frontend (employee PWA mobile viewport 390x844, TEST50 session): with firm bio=true + user fp_required=true, home tab loads normally — fp-unlock-gate is silently skipped because the headless browser has isUserVerifyingPlatformAuthenticatorAvailable()=false (verified via CDP evaluate), no virtual authenticator added. Profile tab shows fingerprint-card with 'REQUIRED BY EMPLOYER' badge and the 'no fingerprint support — normal flow automatically' note. Attendance tab loads and Punch button is visible/tappable (silent fallback). After flipping firm bio_matrix_attendance=false, /auth/me shows effective_fingerprint_required=false and fingerprint-card is HIDDEN on the Profile tab as required. MINOR UI BUG (below): on /employee-master reload the fingerprint toggle does NOT reflect persisted users.fingerprint_required — the employee-detail loader (employee-master.tsx:161-210) never copies fingerprint_required from the /admin/employees list response into the local `emp` object, so the toggle always renders OFF on reload even when the DB has true. All mutations restored: firm bio=false, user.fingerprint_required=false, fingerprint_enrolled_at / fingerprint_device unset.

ui_bugs.issue: After saving the Fingerprint verification toggle, the persisted users.fingerprint_required=true is not reflected on page reload — toggle re-appears as OFF. Root cause: the loader (employee-master.tsx lines 161–210) only copies a whitelisted set of keys (employee_type, is_onroll, bio_code, address, present_address, uan_no, esi_ip_no) from the /admin/employees list item onto the local `emp` object; fingerprint_required (and fingerprint_enrolled_at / fingerprint_device) are NOT copied, so `(emp as any).fingerprint_required` is always undefined in EmployeeGroupingCard's initial state (line 1120-1121). API layer is correct — GET /api/admin/employees does return fingerprint_required=true. Fix: add fingerprint_required, fingerprint_enrolled_at, fingerprint_device to the Object.assign block around line 200.

action_items: Fix employee-master.tsx loader to pass fingerprint_required (+ fingerprint_enrolled_at, fingerprint_device) from the /admin/employees list item into the local emp state so the Grouping card reflects saved state after reload. Add: fingerprint_required: full.fingerprint_required === true, fingerprint_enrolled_at: full.fingerprint_enrolled_at ?? null, fingerprint_device: full.fingerprint_device ?? null.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py continues to grow past 19,000 lines — Iter 165 additions in _enrich_user_with_company (~line 2575) and the user-role PATCH gate (~12507) are self-contained and small, but the same code-organisation feedback from Iter 164 still applies: consider extracting salary-process feature gates (_firm_offline_salary_enabled, _firm_biometric_attendance_enabled) into a helpers module.
FingerprintUnlockGate module-level `unlockedThisSession` boolean is fine for a PWA session but survives tab-switches in the same web session; that matches the 'once per app session' spec.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (1 minor UI persistence bug on admin employee-master reload)

seed_data_creation: None permanent. Temporary mutations (firm_masters bio_matrix_attendance=true, users.fingerprint_required=true, fingerprint_enrolled_at/device set) were made during testing and all restored to the pre-test state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 165 backend fully green (7/7 pytest). All Mongo state restored (cmp_527fecdd7c salary_process.bio_matrix_attendance=false, user_44cd6f561da0 fingerprint_required=false, fingerprint_enrolled_at/device unset). One UI bug pending: employee-master.tsx doesn't hydrate fingerprint_required from /admin/employees list so the toggle appears OFF after reload even when persisted true. WebAuthn gate (fp-unlock-gate) verified only via silent-fallback path — CDP addVirtualAuthenticator not exercised in this run; if next iteration wants to see the real WebAuthn enroll/verify UI use page.context.new_cdp_session then send WebAuthn.enable + WebAuthn.addVirtualAuthenticator {protocol:'ctap2', transport:'internal', hasUserVerification:true, isUserVerified:true}. Employee login: POST /api/auth/pin-login {login_id:'TEST50', pin:'123456'} still works.
rca_of_issue: Employee-master reload showing OFF toggle after save: /admin/employees returns fingerprint_required correctly (verified via curl), but /app/frontend/app/employee-master.tsx `load()` function selectively Object.assigns fields from `full` into `e`, and fingerprint_required is missing from that whitelist (see lines 176-210). EmployeeGroupingCard's useState<boolean>((emp as any).fingerprint_required === true) therefore always initialises to false on reload. One-line fix in the Object.assign block.
